nodes.title: Validate input and handle empty array early
nodes.why: A clear guard makes behavior predictable: wrong type gives TypeError, empty input gives AggregateError.
nodes.alternative: Skip guards and let runtime failures happen deeper in the logic.
nodes.tradeoff: A few extra lines, but far better developer feedback and spec-like behavior.

nodes.title: Resolve immediately when any promise fulfills
nodes.why: Promise.any is first-success wins, so we should stop waiting once one value fulfills.
nodes.alternative: Wait for every promise to settle before deciding.
nodes.tradeoff: Fast success path, but pending tasks may continue running in background.

nodes.title: Collect rejection reasons and fail only when all reject
nodes.why: promiseAny should ignore early failures and reject only after every input has failed.
nodes.alternative: Reject on the first error (Promise.race-like behavior).
nodes.tradeoff: Correct Promise.any semantics, at the cost of tracking counters and error arrays.
